threats.title: Weak Authentication Mechanism
threats.description: The system lacks detailed information about its authentication mechanisms. Without knowing the specific methods employed (e.g., password complexity, MFA, SSO), attackers can potentially bypass authentication and gain unauthorized access to the system. This could involve brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication logic itself.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting weak password policies, lack of MFA, or vulnerabilities in the authentication code itself.  Potentially leveraging stolen credentials from other systems.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Enforce strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation).
Regularly review and update authentication protocols and libraries.

threats.title: Insufficient Authorization Controls
threats.description: The absence of details regarding authorization mechanisms creates a significant risk of privilege escalation. Attackers could potentially exploit vulnerabilities to gain elevated privileges, allowing them to perform actions beyond their intended scope and compromise the entire system.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the authorization logic, bypassing access controls, or leveraging default credentials.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization controls based on the principle of least privilege.
Regularly review and update authorization policies.
Conduct penetration testing to identify and remediate authorization vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion Vulnerability
threats.description: The system's lack of information about its architecture and potential attack vectors makes it susceptible to denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. Attackers could exploit vulnerabilities to consume excessive system resources, leading to service outages and impacting legitimate users.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the system’s code or infrastructure, sending malicious requests, or leveraging known vulnerabilities to overwhelm the system with traffic.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Utilize a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic.
Employ load balancing to distribute traffic across multiple servers.

threats.title: Data Injection Vulnerability
threats.description: Due to the lack of information regarding input validation and sanitization, the system is vulnerable to data injection attacks. Attackers can inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting) into input fields, potentially altering data, executing arbitrary commands, or compromising the system.
threats.attack_vector: Malicious input crafted by an attacker, bypassing input validation and sanitization routines.  This could involve injecting SQL commands, JavaScript code, or other harmful payloads.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization for all user inputs.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic.

threats.title: Lack of Data Classification
threats.description: Without knowing the sensitivity of the data stored and processed by the system, it's impossible to accurately assess the risk of information disclosure. Sensitive data could be exposed through vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, or insider threats, leading to regulatory breaches and reputational damage.
threats.affected_component: All Data Storage and Processing Components
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the system’s code or infrastructure, misconfigured access controls, or malicious insiders gaining unauthorized access to sensitive data.
mitigations: Conduct a data classification exercise to identify and categorize data based on sensitivity.
Implement appropriate access controls based on data classification.
Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit.

gaps: Encryption at rest not fully implemented
priorities: 1. Implement comprehensive input validation (NIST SI-10, ASVS V5)
2. Enable MFA for privileged accounts (NIST IA-2, ASVS V2)
3. Enhance audit logging coverage (NIST AU-2, ASVS V7)
4. Deploy encryption for sensitive data (NIST SC-28, ASVS V6)
5. Strengthen access controls (NIST AC-3, ASVS V4)

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class api_gateway,auth_service,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Mechanism
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insufficient Authorization Controls
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Data Injection Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Lack of Data Classification
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
